commands.runtime_contract: cd Skills/SkillsManager-Doc-Structure && npm run cli -- runtime-contract --json
commands.lint_doc_anchors: cd Skills/SkillsManager-Doc-Structure && npm run cli -- lint-doc-anchors --target <skill_root> --json
commands.lint_split_points: cd Skills/SkillsManager-Doc-Structure && npm run cli -- lint-split-points --target <skill_root> --json
commands.register_split_decision: cd Skills/SkillsManager-Doc-Structure && npm run cli -- register-split-decision --target <skill_root> --doc <doc_path> --rule <rule_id> --decision <accepted|split_required> --note <text> --json
commands.build_anchor_graph: cd Skills/SkillsManager-Doc-Structure && npm run cli -- build-anchor-graph --target <skill_root> --json
commands.rebuild_self_graph: cd Skills/SkillsManager-Doc-Structure && npm run cli -- rebuild-self-graph --json

tool_entry.runtime_entry_rule: Use the TypeScript npm CLI as the full tool surface. The Python wrapper remains contract-only compatibility glue.

must_use_sequence: Read the runtime contract through the CLI JSON entry before deep references.
When the task needs lint, graph rebuild, or split-decision writes, enter through the skill-local npm CLI from the skill root.
Do not assume the Python wrapper exposes lint-doc-anchors or lint-split-points; it only mirrors the contract payload.
Treat SKILL.md as a facade and route deeper only after the contract is known.
Read target SKILL.md skill_mode before enforcing doc-tree lint; guide_only must return a skipped applicability result instead of structural errors.
